title: Nearer, My God, to Thee
firstLine: Nearer, my God, to Thee, nearer to Thee!
publicDomainBasis: Sarah F. Adams (1805–1848), Lowell Mason (1792–1872). Text 1841, tune 1856. Both public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Nearer, my God, to Thee,
Nearer to Thee!
E'en though it be a cross
That raiseth me,
Still all my song shall be,
Nearer, my God, to Thee,
Nearer to Thee!

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Though like the wanderer,
The sun gone down,
Darkness be over me,
My rest a stone,
Yet in my dreams I'd be
Nearer, my God, to Thee,
Nearer to Thee!

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
There let the way appear,
Steps unto heaven;
All that Thou sendest me,
In mercy given;
Angels to beckon me
Nearer, my God, to Thee,
Nearer to Thee!

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
Then, with my waking thoughts
Bright with Thy praise,
Out of my stony griefs
Bethel I'll raise;
So by my woes to be
Nearer, my God, to Thee,
Nearer to Thee!

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
Or if, on joyful wing
Cleaving the sky,
Sun, moon, and stars forgot,
Upward I'll fly,
Still all my song shall be,
Nearer, my God, to Thee,
Nearer to Thee!
